ncpg_affiliate_name: Alabama Council on Problem Gambling and Gaming
primary_prevention_org: Alabama Council on Problem Gambling and Gaming
funding_mechanism: no dedicated funding (no legal commercial casino gaming; affiliate is volunteer-funded)
notes: NCPG page does not list a state-specific helpline; defaults to national 1-800-MY-RESET (24/7).

primary_prevention_org: no NCPG affiliate
funding_mechanism: no dedicated funding
notes: No in-state resources listed on NCPG page. Default national helpline only.

primary_prevention_org: Arizona Department of Gaming - Division of Problem Gambling
funding_mechanism: gaming tax revenue (tribal-state compact contributions to ADG problem gambling fund)
notes: Arizona Department of Gaming Division of Problem Gambling operates 1-800-NEXT-STEP (24/7); NCPG affiliate also active.

primary_prevention_org: Arkansas Problem Gambling Council
funding_mechanism: unknown
notes: NCPG page does not list a state-specific helpline. Affiliate is the in-state contact.

primary_prevention_org: California Office of Problem Gambling (CDPH)
funding_mechanism: tribal-state compact contributions and General Fund appropriations to CDPH Office of Problem Gambling
notes: California Office of Problem Gambling (CDPH) operates state helpline; CalPG is NCPG affiliate.

primary_prevention_org: Problem Gambling Coalition of Colorado
funding_mechanism: limited gaming tax + sports betting tax allocation to Crisis Services Cash Fund
notes: Colorado funds problem gambling through statutory share of sports betting tax revenue (HB19-1327).

primary_prevention_org: CT Department of Mental Health and Addiction Services - Problem Gambling Services
funding_mechanism: Chronic Gamblers Treatment and Rehabilitation Account funded by lottery and gaming revenues (CGS Sec. 17a-713)
notes: CT DMHAS operates 1-888-789-7777 statewide helpline; CCPG is NCPG affiliate.

primary_prevention_org: Delaware Council on Gambling Problems
funding_mechanism: video lottery (racino) revenue set-aside per 29 Del. C. § 4815
notes: DCGP operates 1-888-850-8888; funded via VLT revenue set-aside.

primary_prevention_org: DC Department of Behavioral Health - Office of Problem Gambling
funding_mechanism: DC Lottery and sports wagering revenue allocation
notes: No NCPG affiliate. DC Lottery and DBH provide resources; defaults to national helpline.

primary_prevention_org: Florida Council on Compulsive Gambling
funding_mechanism: no dedicated state appropriation (FCCG is funded primarily through private/industry contributions)
notes: FCCG operates 888-ADMIT-IT 24/7; Florida does not appropriate state funds to the helpline.

primary_prevention_org: Georgia Department of Behavioral Health and Developmental Disabilities
funding_mechanism: no dedicated funding (no legal commercial casino gaming; lottery proceeds dedicated to HOPE/education)
notes: No NCPG affiliate. Georgia Crisis & Access Line (800-715-4225) handles behavioral health referrals.

primary_prevention_org: no NCPG affiliate
funding_mechanism: no dedicated funding (gambling is illegal in Hawaii except limited social gaming)
notes: No legal commercial gambling in HI; no state funding for PG services.

primary_prevention_org: no NCPG affiliate
funding_mechanism: unknown
notes: No NCPG affiliate; tribal gaming compacts may include limited PG funding but not publicly documented.

primary_prevention_org: Illinois Department of Human Services - Division of Substance Use Prevention and Recovery
funding_mechanism: casino/sports wagering tax revenue allocated to Compulsive Gambling Fund (230 ILCS 10/13)
notes: IL DHS/SUPR oversees state PG services; ICPG is NCPG affiliate.

primary_prevention_org: Indiana Family and Social Services Administration - Division of Mental Health and Addiction
funding_mechanism: Indiana Problem Gambling Fund funded by riverboat admission/wagering tax set-aside (IC 4-33-12-6)
notes: IN FSSA/DMHA operates state PG program; 1-800-9-WITH-IT is state helpline.

primary_prevention_org: Iowa Department of Health and Human Services - Office of Gambling Treatment and Prevention
funding_mechanism: gaming tax (Iowa Code 99F.4) - statutory % of casino adjusted gross receipts to Gambling Treatment Fund
notes: Iowa HHS operates 1-800-BETS-OFF; no NCPG affiliate listed but state program is well-established.

primary_prevention_org: Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services - Problem Gambling and Addictions Grant Fund
funding_mechanism: Problem Gambling and Addictions Grant Fund - 2% of state-owned casino revenue (K.S.A. 74-8768)
notes: KDADS administers Problem Gambling and Addictions Fund; KCPG is NCPG affiliate.

primary_prevention_org: Kentucky Council on Problem Gambling
funding_mechanism: sports wagering tax allocation to Problem Gambling Assistance Account (KRS 230.215; HB 551 2023)
notes: KY sports wagering law (2023) created Problem Gambling Assistance Account funded by 2.5% of sports wagering excise tax.

primary_prevention_org: Louisiana Department of Health - Office of Behavioral Health
funding_mechanism: Compulsive and Problem Gambling Fund - riverboat admission fee set-aside (La. R.S. 27:92)
notes: Louisiana operates 1-877-770-STOP statewide; LACG is NCPG affiliate.

ncpg_affiliate_name: Maine Council on Problem Gambling, Inc.
primary_prevention_org: Maine DHHS Office of Behavioral Health - Problem Gambling Program (via Maine CDC)
funding_mechanism: Gambling Addiction Prevention and Treatment Fund (8 M.R.S. §1036) - statutory $100K/yr from Hollywood Casino slot revenue + share of Oxford Casino revenue
annual_budget_disclosed: $100,000+ annually (statutory minimum from slots); FY24 report available via Maine Legislature
notes: James's home state. 211 Maine is the in-state PG helpline (24/7 live staff); 1-800-GAMBLER calls from 207 area code route to 211. Maine CDC/DHHS manages Gambling Addiction Prevention and Treatment Fund. Four no-cost telehealth counseling sessions available statewide to any Mainer (2025 expansion). Maine Council on Problem Gambling is the NCPG affiliate.

primary_prevention_org: Maryland Center of Excellence on Problem Gambling (University of Maryland School of Medicine)
funding_mechanism: Problem Gambling Fund - annual assessment paid by Maryland's six casinos (Md. Code State Gov't §9-1A-33)
notes: MD Center of Excellence on Problem Gambling operates 1-800-GAMBLER (24/7). Fund derived from casino assessments.

ncpg_affiliate_name: Massachusetts Council on Gaming and Health
primary_prevention_org: Massachusetts Department of Public Health - Office of Problem Gambling Services
funding_mechanism: Public Health Trust Fund - 5% of gross casino/sports wagering revenue per M.G.L. c.23K §58 and c.23N
annual_budget_disclosed: $17.3 million (FY23 OPGS final budget); $22.6 million (FY23 NAADGS) -- highest in nation
notes: MA has the largest publicly funded PG program in the country. MA DPH Office of Problem Gambling Services oversees; MACGH is NCPG affiliate.

primary_prevention_org: Michigan Department of Health and Human Services - Office of Recovery Oriented Systems of Care
funding_mechanism: Compulsive Gaming Prevention Fund - statutory share of casino wagering tax (MCL 432.253)
notes: MDHHS operates 1-800-270-7117 24/7. MAPG is NCPG affiliate (no URL on NCPG page).

primary_prevention_org: Minnesota Department of Human Services - Behavioral Health Division
funding_mechanism: Compulsive Gambling Treatment Appropriation (Minn. Stat. §245.98) - lottery in-lieu-of-tax payments
notes: MN DHS operates state PG program; helpline 1-800-333-HOPE supports call and text. MNAPG is NCPG affiliate.

ncpg_affiliate_name: Mississippi Council on Problem and Compulsive Gambling
primary_prevention_org: Mississippi Council on Problem and Compulsive Gambling
funding_mechanism: Mississippi Department of Mental Health appropriation funded by gaming fees
notes: MCPCG operates statewide 24/7 helpline 888-777-9696.

primary_prevention_org: Missouri Department of Mental Health - Division of Behavioral Health
funding_mechanism: Compulsive Gamblers Fund - $1 per casino admission per RSMo 313.842
notes: No NCPG affiliate listed; MO DMH operates 1-888-BETS-OFF statewide.

primary_prevention_org: Montana Council on Problem Gambling
funding_mechanism: no dedicated state funding; MTCPG is a 501(c)(3) supported via grants
notes: MTCPG operates 1-888-900-9979 statewide helpline.

primary_prevention_org: Nebraska Commission on Problem Gambling / Gamblers Assistance Program
funding_mechanism: Compulsive Gamblers Assistance Fund - 0.5% of state lottery proceeds + casino keno fees (Neb. Rev. Stat. §9-1006)
notes: Nebraska Commission on Problem Gambling (state agency) operates Gamblers Assistance Program; NCPG affiliate also active.

primary_prevention_org: Nevada Council on Problem Gambling
funding_mechanism: Revolving Account for Problem Gambling - $2 per slot machine fee per NRS 463.385 + NRS 458A
notes: Nevada Council on Problem Gambling is NCPG affiliate; state PG fund administered by Nevada DHHS.

primary_prevention_org: New Hampshire Council on Problem Gambling (independent 501c3)
funding_mechanism: Charitable Gaming Revenue + sports wagering (NH RSA 287-I provides for treatment fund)
notes: No NCPG affiliate listed. NHCPG (separate org) exists; state helpline coverage relies on 211NH and national 1-800-MY-RESET (24/7).

ncpg_affiliate_name: no NCPG affiliate listed on NCPG state page
primary_prevention_org: Council on Compulsive Gambling of New Jersey
funding_mechanism: Compulsive Gambling Treatment and Prevention Fund (NJSA 5:12-148) - casino/sports betting taxes
notes: NJ originated 1-800-GAMBLER. Council on Compulsive Gambling of NJ operates it but NCPG page does not list a NJ affiliate (CCGNJ separated from NCPG affiliation).

ncpg_affiliate_name: New Mexico Council on Problem Gambling
primary_prevention_org: Responsible Gaming Association of New Mexico
funding_mechanism: tribal-state compact contributions + Indian Gaming Compact set-asides
notes: NMCPG is NCPG affiliate; RGANM coordinates industry funding.

ncpg_affiliate_name: New York Council on Problem Gambling
primary_prevention_org: New York State Office of Addiction Services and Supports (OASAS)
funding_mechanism: Problem Gambling Education and Prevention Fund - share of mobile sports wagering tax revenue (Tax Law §1612)
notes: NYS OASAS operates HOPEline 24/7; NYCPG is NCPG affiliate.

ncpg_affiliate_name: North Carolina Council on Problem Gambling
primary_prevention_org: NC Department of Health and Human Services - Problem Gambling Program
funding_mechanism: Education Lottery proceeds set-aside (N.C.G.S. 18C-163) + sports wagering tax (SB 38 2023)
notes: NC DHHS operates 1-877-718-5543 statewide. NCCPG is NCPG affiliate (no URL on NCPG page).

primary_prevention_org: North Dakota Department of Health and Human Services - Behavioral Health Division
funding_mechanism: Charitable gaming tax revenue allocation (N.D.C.C. ch. 53-06.1)
notes: No NCPG affiliate listed. ND HHS Behavioral Health Division provides state PG services.

ncpg_affiliate_name: The Problem Gambling Network of Ohio
primary_prevention_org: Ohio for Responsible Gambling (interagency coalition) + Ohio Department of Mental Health and Addiction Services
funding_mechanism: Problem Casino Gambling and Addictions Fund + Problem Sports Gaming Fund - 2% of casino tax + 2% of sports gaming tax (ORC 5753.03 and 3772.36)
annual_budget_disclosed: $1 million contract to PGNOhio through 6/30/2027 (OhioMHAS award)
notes: James's working jurisdiction. Ohio for Responsible Gambling (ORG) is a 4-agency coalition: Ohio Lottery, Casino Control Commission, State Racing Commission, OhioMHAS. State helpline 1-800-589-9966 is 24/7. PGNOhio is the NCPG-affiliated nonprofit and recently received $1M contract from OhioMHAS through June 30, 2027. Sports gaming law allocates 2% of tax to Problem Sports Gaming Fund.

ncpg_affiliate_name: Oklahoma Association on Problem Gambling and Gaming
primary_prevention_org: Oklahoma Department of Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services
funding_mechanism: tribal-state compact contributions (Section 11(B) of Model Tribal Gaming Compact) + ODMHSAS appropriations
notes: ODMHSAS administers state PG program; OAPGG is NCPG affiliate.

primary_prevention_org: Oregon Health Authority - Problem Gambling Services
funding_mechanism: Oregon Lottery revenue set-aside - 1% of net lottery proceeds per ORS 461.549
notes: Oregon has one of the longest-running and best-funded state PG programs; 1% of lottery revenue is statutorily dedicated.

ncpg_affiliate_name: Council on Compulsive Gambling of Pennsylvania, Inc.
primary_prevention_org: PA Department of Drug and Alcohol Programs
funding_mechanism: Compulsive and Problem Gambling Treatment Fund - $2 million minimum from each licensed slot machine licensee (4 Pa.C.S. §1509)
notes: PA DDAP administers Compulsive and Problem Gambling Treatment Fund; CCGPA is NCPG affiliate.

ncpg_affiliate_name: Rhode Island Council on Problem Gambling
primary_prevention_org: Rhode Island Council on Problem Gambling
funding_mechanism: RI Lottery (state-owned VLT operations) appropriations to BHDDH
notes: RICPG operates helpline 1-877-942-6253.

primary_prevention_org: South Carolina Department of Mental Health
funding_mechanism: no dedicated funding (no legal commercial casino gaming; lottery proceeds dedicated to education)
notes: No NCPG affiliate. Limited state-funded PG services.

primary_prevention_org: SD Department of Social Services - Division of Behavioral Health
funding_mechanism: Gaming Commission Fund + lottery proceeds (SDCL 42-7B-48.1)
notes: No NCPG affiliate. SD DSS Behavioral Health operates state PG treatment program.

primary_prevention_org: Tennessee Sports Wagering Council + TAADAS (Tennessee Association of Alcohol Drug and other Addiction Services)
funding_mechanism: Problem Gambling Fund - statutory share of sports wagering privilege tax per Tenn. Code §4-49-118
notes: No NCPG affiliate listed on NCPG state page (Texas/Vermont were 2024 additions; TN status pending). TN REDLINE handles PG referrals.

primary_prevention_org: Texas Coalition on Problem Gambling
funding_mechanism: no dedicated state funding (no legal commercial casino gaming; lottery sets aside small responsible gambling allocation)
notes: TXCPG was added as NCPG affiliate in 2024.

primary_prevention_org: no NCPG affiliate
funding_mechanism: no dedicated funding (gambling is illegal in Utah; constitutional prohibition)
notes: No legal gambling in UT; no state PG program.

ncpg_affiliate_name: Vermont Council on Gaming and Health
primary_prevention_org: Vermont Department of Mental Health
funding_mechanism: Sports wagering tax allocation (Act 63 of 2023; 31 V.S.A. §1606)
notes: VT Council on Gaming and Health was added as NCPG affiliate in 2024 after VT legalized sports betting (Act 63 of 2023).

primary_prevention_org: Virginia Department of Behavioral Health and Developmental Services - Problem Gambling Treatment and Support Fund
funding_mechanism: Problem Gambling Treatment and Support Fund - 2.5% of sports betting tax + share of casino tax (Va. Code §37.2-314.2)
notes: VA DBHDS administers Problem Gambling Treatment and Support Fund; VCPG operates the helpline.

primary_prevention_org: Washington State Health Care Authority - Division of Behavioral Health and Recovery
funding_mechanism: Business and Occupation tax on gambling activities + tribal-state compact contributions (RCW 43.20A.890)
notes: Evergreen Council on Problem Gambling operates 1-800-547-6133 (call or text 24/7); WA HCA funds state PG program via B&O tax.

primary_prevention_org: WV Problem Gamblers Help Network / First Choice Services
funding_mechanism: Compulsive Gambling Treatment Fund - statutory share of video lottery and racetrack VLT revenue (W. Va. Code §29-22A-19)
notes: No NCPG affiliate. WV Problem Gamblers Help Network (First Choice Services) operates 1-800-GAMBLER state line.

primary_prevention_org: Wisconsin Council on Problem Gambling
funding_mechanism: Wisconsin Indian Gaming Association contributions + tribal-state compact set-asides; no general fund appropriation
notes: WCPG operates statewide 24/7 helpline 1-800-426-2535 funded largely by Wisconsin tribal gaming contributions.

primary_prevention_org: Wyoming Council on Problem Gambling
funding_mechanism: Wyoming Gaming Commission set-aside from online sports wagering tax (W.S. 9-24-104)
notes: WYCPG is NCPG affiliate; small state program; sports wagering law (2021) created PG funding mechanism.

notes: No AS-specific NCPG affiliate or in-territory PG resources listed.
